headline: Model Context Protocol and agent orchestration infrastructure broke out across the window as the connective layer for multi-agent systems.
body: Model Context Protocol led the breakouts with a cluster of items defining its client-server architecture, JSON-RPC messaging, and enterprise grounding through tools like the Agent Development Kit and Looker. A parallel push toward orchestration appeared in Cursor's account of running 128 coding agents at once and WorkOS's offline workflow that dispatches agents via voice briefs into isolated git worktrees with signal agents and verification gates. New entrants voice input, git worktree isolation, and signal agents all trace to that asynchronous agent design. Anthropic's Fable 5 surfaced repeatedly as a coding and content-production model, paired with WebMCP, a proposed web standard exposing typed tools to agents in early preview on Chrome 146.

cited_items.title: Your first agent configuration with MCP and Looker
